channel.title: Daily AI Digest

item.title: Gated DeltaNet-2: Decoupling Erase and Write in Linear Attention
item.description: 📄 New in cs.AI — Linear attention replaces the unbounded cache of softmax attention with a fixed-size recurrent state, reducing sequence mixing to linear time and decoding to constant memory. The hard part is not just what to forget, but how to edit this compressed memory without scrambling existing associations....

item.title: Sensor2Sensor: Cross-Embodiment Sensor Conversion for Autonomous Driving
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV — Robust training and validation of Autonomous Driving Systems (ADS) require massive, diverse datasets. Proprietary data collected by Autonomous Vehicle (AV) fleets, while high-fidelity, are limited in scale, diversity of sensor configurations, as well as geographic and long-tail-behavioral coverag...

item.title: WorldKV: Efficient World Memory with World Retrieval and Compression
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV — Autoregressive video diffusion models have enabled real-time, action-conditioned world generation. However, sustaining a persistent world, where revisiting a previously seen viewpoint yields consistent content, remains an open problem. Full KV-cache attention preserves this consistency but breaks...

item.title: Swift Sampling: Selecting Temporal Surprises via Taylor Series
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV, cs.AI — While most frames in long-form video are redundant, the critical information resides in temporal surprises: moments where the actual visual features deviate from their predicted evolution. Inspired by the human brain's predictive coding, we introduce Swift Sampling, an elegant, training-free fram...

item.title: SEGA: Spectral-Energy Guided Attention for Resolution Extrapolation in Diffusion Transformers
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV — Diffusion transformers (DiTs) have emerged as a dominant architecture for text-to-image generation, yet their performance drops when generating at resolutions beyond their training range. Existing training-free approaches mitigate this by modifying inference-time attention behavior, often through...

item.title: The Matching Principle: A Geometric Theory of Loss Functions for Nuisance-Robust Representation Learning
item.description: 📄 New in cs.LG, cs.AI — Robustness, domain adaptation, photometric and occlusion invariance, compositional generalisation, temporal robustness, alignment safety, and classical anisotropic regularisation are usually treated as separate problems with separate method families. This paper argues that much of their shared st...

item.title: Branch-Stochastic Model Predictive Control for Motion Planning under Multi-Modal Uncertainty with Scenario Clustering
item.description: 📄 New in cs.RO — Motion planning for autonomous driving must account for multi-modal uncertainty in both the intentions and trajectories of surrounding vehicles. Handling uncertainty in a worst-case manner guarantees robustness but often leads to excessive conservatism. Stochastic Model Predictive Control (SMPC) ...

item.title: evidentlyai / evidently
item.description: 💻 Trending on GitHub — Evidently is ​​an open-source ML and LLM observability framework. Evaluate, test, and monitor any AI-powered system or data pipeline. From tabular data to Gen AI. 100+ metrics.

item.title: When Space Begins to See: WiFi, AI, and the New Form of Invisible Surveillance

item.title: plastic-labs / honcho
item.description: ⭐ 133 GitHub stars today — Memory library for building stateful agents

item.title: microsoft / agent-governance-toolkit
item.description: ⭐ 86 stars today on GitHub — AI Agent Governance Toolkit — Policy enforcement, zero-trust identity, execution sandboxing, and reliability engineering for autonomous AI agents. Covers 10/10 OWASP Agentic Top 10.
